mission.title: Install and Configure HwameiStor on Kubernetes
mission.description: HwameiStor is a Kubernetes-native HA local storage system for cloud-native stateful workloads. It creates a local storage resource pool from node disks and provides distributed data persistence through CSI volumes. The HwameiStor Operator deploys and manages all components automatically.

steps.title: Install node prerequisites
steps.description:
HwameiStor requires LVM2 on all storage nodes. Install the required packages on each node:
```bash
# Debian/Ubuntu
apt-get install -y lvm2
# RHEL/CentOS
yum install -y lvm2
```
Each storage node must also have at least one unused (unpartitioned, unmounted) disk available.

steps.title: Add the HwameiStor Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the HwameiStor Operator Helm chart repository:
```bash
helm repo add hwameistor-operator https://hwameistor.io/hwameistor-operator
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install HwameiStor using Helm
steps.description:
Install the HwameiStor Operator, which will deploy all components:
```bash
helm install hwameistor-operator hwameistor-operator/hwameistor-operator -n hwameistor --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that all HwameiStor pods are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n hwameistor
```
You should see the operator, local-storage, local-disk-manager, scheduler, admission-controller, and evictor pods.

steps.title: Verify local disks are claimed
steps.description:
Check that HwameiStor has detected the available disks on your nodes:
```bash
kubectl get localdisks
kubectl get localdisknodes
```
Available disks should show as `Unclaimed` or `Claimed` depending on auto-claim settings.

resolution.summary: A successful installation will show all HwameiStor pods running in the hwameistor namespace (operator, local-storage, local-disk-manager, scheduler, admission-controller, evictor). Local disks will be detected and StorageClasses will be available for PVC provisioning.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add hwameistor-operator https://hwameistor.io/hwameistor-operator
helm repo update

uninstall.title: Remove HwameiStor
uninstall.description:
First ensure no PVCs are using HwameiStor volumes, then uninstall:
```bash
helm uninstall hwameistor-operator -n hwameistor
kubectl get crd -o name | grep hwameistor.io | xargs kubectl delete
kubectl delete namespace hwameistor
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade HwameiStor
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade the operator:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade hwameistor-operator hwameistor-operator/hwameistor-operator -n hwameistor
kubectl get pods -n hwameistor
```

troubleshooting.title: Local-disk-manager pods not starting
troubleshooting.description:
The local-disk-manager runs as a DaemonSet and requires LVM2 on each node. Check if LVM2 is installed:
```bash
kubectl logs -n hwameistor -l app=hwameistor-local-disk-manager --tail=50
```

troubleshooting.title: No disks detected
troubleshooting.description:
Verify that nodes have unused disks available (no partitions, not mounted):
```bash
kubectl get localdisks -o wide
kubectl get localdisknodes
```
Disks that are already partitioned or mounted will not be detected.

troubleshooting.title: StorageClass not available
troubleshooting.description:
Check that the operator has created the default StorageClasses:
```bash
kubectl get sc | grep hwameistor
kubectl describe cluster.hwameistor.io
```

prerequisites.description: Requires a Kubernetes cluster with LVM2 installed on all storage nodes. Each node must have at least one unused (unpartitioned, unmounted) disk. Kernel headers are recommended for best compatibility.
